misconceptionSummary: Students know the words but do not connect them to the structure of an expression.

steps.narration: Think of an expression as a complete mathematical phrase. It is the whole thing, not just one part of it.
Terms are the pieces that make up the expression. Plus and minus signs split the expression into separate terms.
Inside the term 4y, the variable is y and the coefficient is 4. The coefficient tells us how many of the variable we have.

visuals.ruleText: variable = unknown letter   coefficient = number multiplying the variable   term = one part   expression = the whole algebraic phrase
visuals.subText: The words describe different jobs inside the same expression

steps.narration: These words are linked. A variable is a letter, a coefficient is the number multiplying it, a term is one part, and the expression is the complete phrase.

visuals.expression: 4y - 7: variable y, coefficient 4, terms 4y and 7, expression 4y - 7
visuals.label: Use the structure of the expression to name each part

steps.narration: When you understand the structure, the terminology becomes clear. Each word names a different feature of the expression.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: memorising the words without seeing the structure. Terms are parts, coefficients are numbers attached to variables, and the expression is the full statement.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Do not treat the words as random labels. They describe the job each part is doing inside the expression.
